arachne has more general use for teams because of her pursuit, and her exalt can do a Lot of shield, but castor is a really good shielder and even at e0 can still do a lot (especially since he applies corrosion)
in the end it's really up to what team(s) you'd be using them in

Sorry I’m new but is running 2/2 much worse than 3/1 ?

this may just be my bias, but doesn't castor provide better shield on a general level? arachne's exalt does a big burst of shield, but you're kind of incentivised to hold off doing it unless you're in ultra round or have done annihilation, whereas castor's exalt does less flat shield but gives you plumes which provide shield later, plus he can boost the damage/crit of other teammates
if youre talking about realm mixing theres no difference
he does provide more turn to turn shield but hes also much slower so if you dont long to kill you also need less shield
plus arachne is just universally way better

Hey. I just got pontos, what team should I run for him? Thanks in advance

Good or bad team:
GHelot/Hameln/Lotan/Faint

Hameln is alright with GHelot, Lotan doesn't really do anything for her, Faint is fine as a 4th Slot but that's usually considering everybody else can Support her in some kind of way such as Aigis Vuln / Stun Support or Thais shenanigans
So replace Lotan with Aigis, got it

I feel like Hameln is best used in teams where double plays are very valuable, but then you need stall and hand space/card draw, but then a team that would want double play would usually have expensive cards, which means high arithmetica requirements

his crescendo also feels a lot like a nothing burger until you stall it out and have a ton of it so you can spam it, but by then... there's nothing left to attach Rondino onto
Does Ghelot's Devour effect also reduce the cost increase from Hameln's Ascending Scale?
like, a 0 cost flail, ascending scale = 2 cost, would a Scion devour make it 0 cost again?
Yes
In fact the game stores negative arithmetica costs but display them as 0

i have 4 prototypes idm using if necessary
